title: Metastelma berteroanum
taxonomy: Metastelma berteroanum belongs to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This classification is due to its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the same family. Metastelma berteroanum is a species of flowering plant that is native to the Hawaiian Islands and other Pacific island regions. It is a member of the genus Metastelma, which consists of about 50 species of flowering plants. The classification of Metastelma berteroanum into the family Apocynaceae highlights its evolutionary connections with other plants in this family.
uses: Metastelma berteroanum, a species of plant native to Hawaii, has been found to have various medicinal properties. Its roots, leaves, and stems are used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective against infections and skin conditions. constituents:

intro: Metastelma berteroanum, also known as Hawaiian milkwort, is a perennial herb native to the Hawaiian Islands, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. Bioactive constituents of Metastelma berteroanum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, while in modern herbalism, it is commonly formulated into topical creams, salves, and ointments to leverage its therapeutic benefits.
